The system is a hospital triage queue system.
The system is for emergency room intake.
The system prioritizes patients based on clinical urgency.
The system ensures critical cases are surfaced to medical staff immediately.
The system ensures the waiting queue always reflects current patient conditions.
The system operates in a high-pressure environment.
The system supports registering incoming patients with their name.
The system supports registering incoming patients with their presenting symptoms.
The system assigns each patient an urgency level from 1 (critical) to 5 (non-urgent) based on symptoms.
The system orders the queue by urgency level first.
The system orders the queue by arrival time within the same urgency level.
The system reorders the queue immediately when a new patient arrives.
The system reorders the queue immediately when an existing patient is re-triaged to a different urgency level.
The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.
Taking the patient removes them from the queue.
The system shows a live dashboard of the queue.
The dashboard includes each patient's urgency.
The dashboard includes each patient's waiting time.
The emergency room intake system assigns treatment priority based on the severity of a patient's medical condition.
This platform continuously reorders the waiting list so that the most critical patients are seen first.
Medical staff can view a live dashboard that tracks each patient's urgency level and how long they have been waiting.
When a new patient is registered or an existing patient's condition changes, the queue is instantly updated to reflect the current clinical priorities.
The triage queue management tool organizes the care pathway by ranking patients according to their need for immediate attention.
